col-gha.title: Colombia–Ghana: the Black Stars want to reignite the memory
col-gha.angle: Colombia brings pace and sharp attacking talent. Ghana brings a World Cup memory too heavy to be treated as a simple outsider story.
body: Colombia–Ghana is a match of rhythm, talent and memory. Colombia arrive with Luis Díaz, intensity, acceleration and the ability to turn one action into a storm.
But Ghana are not just another World Cup opponent. The Black Stars carry 2006, 2010, 2014, Abedi Pelé’s legacy and Asamoah Gyan’s generation — a history that still weighs heavily in knockout nights.
Today, Mohammed Kudus embodies a new kind of energy: modern, explosive, capable of breaking lines and giving Ghana the unpredictability that can terrify a favourite.
Colombia will have their weapons. Ghana will have their memory. And sometimes, in a World Cup, memory weighs as much as a game plan.

porcro-spain.title: Portugal/Croatia–Spain: La Roja await their next test
porcro-spain.angle: Spain confirmed their status against Austria. Now they wait for the winner of a hard-edged duel between Portugal and Croatia.
body: Spain did the job: a 3–0 win over Austria, a clean qualification, and the sense that La Roja are warming up at the right time.
What comes next will feel different. Portugal or Croatia would mean an opponent used to major nights, capable of turning a controlled evening into a battle of nerves.

For Spain, the challenge is simple: keep control without getting comfortable. When La Roja dictate the rhythm, they can suffocate anyone. But at this stage, possession is not enough: they must also close the door, strike at the right moment and survive difficult spells.
Spain are in the last 16. The control is there. Now they must prove it can carry them into the rounds that truly matter.
